primitive.comments: Absolute value of a signed 32-bit two's-complement integer. For non-negative inputs the result equals the input; for negative inputs the result is the arithmetic negation. The function is total but not injective at the boundary: abs(minBound) = minBound (i.e. abs(-2147483648) = -2147483648), because +2147483648 is not representable in int32. Corresponds to Haskell's abs :: Int32 -> Int32.
primitive.description: The absolute value of an integer.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.abs

primitive.comments: Principal value of the inverse cosine, in radians. The result is in [0, π]. For arguments outside the domain [-1, +1] (including ±∞), the result is NaN. acos(NaN) is NaN. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੹.2 acos operation and to Haskell's acos :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The arc cosine of a floating-point number.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.acos

primitive.comments: Principal value of the inverse hyperbolic cosine. The result is in [0, +∞). For arguments less than 1 the result is NaN; acosh(1) = +0; acosh(+∞) = +∞; acosh(NaN) is NaN. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੹.2 acosh operation and to Haskell's acosh :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The hyperbolic arc cosine of a floating-point number.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.acosh

primitive.comments: Two's-complement 32-bit signed integer addition. The result is x + y reduced modulo 2^32 and reinterpreted as a signed int32 (i.e. arithmetic wraps silently on overflow, with no exception raised). The operation is total. Corresponds to Haskell's (+) :: Int32 -> Int32 -> Int32.
primitive.description: Integer addition.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.add

primitive.comments: IEEE 754 binary64 addition. The result is the value of x + y rounded to the nearest representable float64 under the roundTiesToEven rounding-direction attribute. Adding infinities of opposite sign (+∞ + -∞ or -∞ + +∞) produces a NaN; adding any value to NaN produces a NaN. The sum of two zeros is +0, except (-0) + (-0) = -0. The operation is total: it never raises, but it may produce NaN. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੵ.4.1 addition operation and to Haskell's (+) :: Double -> Double -> Double.
primitive.description: Floating-point addition.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.addFloat64

primitive.comments: Principal value of the inverse sine, in radians. The result is in [-π/2, +π/2]. For arguments outside the domain [-1, +1] (including ±∞), the result is NaN; asin(ଐ) = ଐ; asin(NaN) is NaN. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੹.2 asin operation and to Haskell's asin :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The arc sine of a floating-point number.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.asin

primitive.comments: Principal value of the inverse hyperbolic sine. Defined for all finite reals; asinh(ଐ) = ଐ; asinh(±∞) = ±∞; asinh(NaN) is NaN. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੹.2 asinh operation and to Haskell's asinh :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The hyperbolic arc sine of a floating-point number.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.asinh

primitive.comments: Principal value of the inverse tangent, in radians. The result is in (-π/2, +π/2); atan(ଐ) = ଐ; atan(±∞) = ±π/2; atan(NaN) is NaN. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੹.2 atan operation and to Haskell's atan :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The arc tangent of a floating-point number.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.atan

primitive.comments: atan2(y, x) returns the angle in radians, in (-π, +π], from the positive x-axis to the point (x, y), using the signs of both arguments to determine the quadrant. Special cases follow IEEE 754 ੹.2: atan2(ଐ, +x>0) = ଐ; atan2(ଐ, -x<0) = ±π; atan2(±y>0, 0) = ±π/2; atan2(±y, +∞) = ଐ; atan2(±y, -∞) = ±π; atan2(±∞, finite) = ±π/2; atan2(±∞, +∞) = ±π/4; atan2(±∞, -∞) = ଓπ/4; atan2 of any NaN argument is NaN. Corresponds to Haskell's atan2 :: Double -> Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The two-argument arc tangent (atan2).
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.atan2

primitive.comments: Principal value of the inverse hyperbolic tangent. Defined for arguments in (-1, +1); atanh(଑) = ±∞ (with division-by-zero exception in IEEE 754); arguments outside [-1, +1] produce NaN; atanh(ଐ) = ଐ; atanh(NaN) is NaN. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੹.2 atanh operation and to Haskell's atanh :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The hyperbolic arc tangent of a floating-point number.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.atanh

primitive.comments: The smallest integer value not less than the argument, returned as a float64. Equivalent to IEEE 754 ੵ.3.1 roundToIntegralTowardPositive: ceiling(ଐ) = ଐ; ceiling(±∞) = ±∞; ceiling(NaN) is NaN; the sign of a negative result is preserved when rounding to zero (e.g. ceiling(-0.5) = -0). Note that the return type is float64, not an integer type, so the result can exceed the integer range. Corresponds to Haskell's fromIntegral . ceiling :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The smallest integer greater than or equal to the argument, as a float.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.ceiling

primitive.comments: Cosine of an angle in radians, correctly rounded for finite arguments. The result is in [-1, +1]; cos(ଐ) = 1; cos(±∞) is NaN (with invalid-operation exception in IEEE 754); cos(NaN) is NaN. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੹.2 cos operation and to Haskell's cos :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The cosine of a floating-point number.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.cos

primitive.comments: Hyperbolic cosine. The result is in [1, +∞]; cosh(ଐ) = 1; cosh(±∞) = +∞; cosh(NaN) is NaN. Large-magnitude arguments overflow to +∞. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੹.2 cosh operation and to Haskell's cosh :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The hyperbolic cosine of a floating-point number.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.cosh

primitive.comments: The mathematical constant e ≈ 2.718281828459045, the base of the natural logarithm, as the nearest representable float64. Equal to exp(1). Corresponds to Haskell's exp 1 :: Double.
primitive.description: Euler's constant (the base of the natural logarithm).
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.e

primitive.comments: True if the argument is divisible by 2 (i.e. x mod 2 = 0), false otherwise. Total on all int32 inputs including negative numbers and minBound. Corresponds to Haskell's even :: Int32 -> Bool.

literal.string: Test whether an integer is even, defined via maybeMod and equality.

primitive.description: Test whether an integer is even.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.even

primitive.comments: The exponential function: exp(x) = e^x. exp(ଐ) = 1; exp(-∞) = +0; exp(+∞) = +∞; exp(NaN) is NaN. Large positive arguments overflow to +∞; large negative arguments underflow to +0. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੹.2 exp operation and to Haskell's exp :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The exponential function.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.exp

primitive.comments: The largest integer value not greater than the argument, returned as a float64. Equivalent to IEEE 754 ੵ.3.1 roundToIntegralTowardNegative: floor(ଐ) = ଐ; floor(±∞) = ±∞; floor(NaN) is NaN. Note that the return type is float64, not an integer type, so the result can exceed the integer range. Corresponds to Haskell's fromIntegral . floor :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The largest integer less than or equal to the argument, as a float.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.floor

primitive.comments: The natural (base-e) logarithm. log(1) = +0; log(+0) = log(-0) = -∞ (with division-by-zero exception in IEEE 754); log(x) for x < 0 is NaN (with invalid-operation exception); log(+∞) = +∞; log(NaN) is NaN. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੹.2 log operation and to Haskell's log :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The natural logarithm.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.log

primitive.comments: logBase(b, x) computes the logarithm of x in base b, equivalent to log(x) / log(b). Inherits the special-case behavior of log for each argument (NaN propagation, sign of zeros, division by zero on log of a zero, NaN on negative arguments). Corresponds to Haskell's logBase :: Double -> Double -> Double.
primitive.description: Logarithm of the second argument in the base of the first.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.logBase

primitive.comments: Return the larger of two int32 values under the usual signed-integer total order. Total on all inputs. Corresponds to Haskell's max :: Int32 -> Int32 -> Int32.
primitive.description: The maximum of two integers.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.max

primitive.comments: Total integer division: maybeDiv(x, y) returns Just(x divided by y, rounded toward negative infinity) when y is non-zero, and Nothing when y = 0. The division rounds toward negative infinity (floor), so for example maybeDiv(-7, 2) = Just(-4). The boundary case maybeDiv(minBound, -1), whose mathematical result +2147483648 is not representable in int32, wraps to minBound (the two's-complement overflow). Corresponds to Haskell's div :: Int32 -> Int32 -> Int32, wrapped in maybe to make the zero-divisor case total.
primitive.description: Integer division, or Nothing if dividing by zero.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.maybeDiv

primitive.comments: Total integer modulus: maybeMod(x, y) returns Just(x mod y) when y is non-zero, and Nothing when y = 0. The result satisfies the identity x = (maybeDiv(x, y) result) * y + (maybeMod(x, y) result), so the sign of the result matches the sign of y (Knuth-style floor division). For example maybeMod(-7, 2) = Just(1). Corresponds to Haskell's mod :: Int32 -> Int32 -> Int32, wrapped in maybe to make the zero-divisor case total.
primitive.description: Integer modulus, or Nothing if dividing by zero.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.maybeMod

primitive.comments: maybePred(x) returns Just(x - 1) when x > minBound, and Nothing when x = minBound (i.e. -2147483648). The function is total and does not wrap. Corresponds to Haskell's pred :: Int32 -> Int32, wrapped in maybe to make the boundary case total (Haskell's pred is a partial function that raises an error on minBound).
primitive.description: The predecessor of an integer, or Nothing on underflow.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.maybePred

primitive.comments: Total integer remainder: maybeRem(x, y) returns Just(x rem y) when y is non-zero, and Nothing when y = 0. The result satisfies x = (truncate(x / y)) * y + (maybeRem(x, y) result), so the sign of the result matches the sign of x (truncated division, C-style remainder). For example maybeRem(-7, 2) = Just(-1). The boundary case maybeRem(minBound, -1) is 0 (no overflow, since the quotient overflow is absorbed). Corresponds to Haskell's rem :: Int32 -> Int32 -> Int32, wrapped in maybe to make the zero-divisor case total.
primitive.description: Integer remainder, or Nothing if dividing by zero.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.maybeRem

primitive.comments: maybeSucc(x) returns Just(x + 1) when x < maxBound, and Nothing when x = maxBound (i.e. 2147483647). The function is total and does not wrap. Corresponds to Haskell's succ :: Int32 -> Int32, wrapped in maybe to make the boundary case total (Haskell's succ is a partial function that raises an error on maxBound).
primitive.description: The successor of an integer, or Nothing on overflow.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.maybeSucc

primitive.comments: Return the smaller of two int32 values under the usual signed-integer total order. Total on all inputs. Corresponds to Haskell's min :: Int32 -> Int32 -> Int32.
primitive.description: The minimum of two integers.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.min

primitive.comments: Two's-complement 32-bit signed integer multiplication. The result is x * y reduced modulo 2^32 and reinterpreted as a signed int32 (i.e. arithmetic wraps silently on overflow, with no exception raised). The operation is total. Corresponds to Haskell's (*) :: Int32 -> Int32 -> Int32.
primitive.description: Integer multiplication.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.mul

primitive.comments: IEEE 754 binary64 multiplication. The result is the value of x * y rounded to the nearest representable float64 under the roundTiesToEven rounding-direction attribute. Multiplying 0 by ±∞ (in either order) produces a NaN (with invalid-operation exception in IEEE 754); multiplying any value by NaN produces a NaN. The result's sign is the XOR of the operand signs (ଐ * ଐ = ଐ accordingly). The operation is total: it never raises, but it may produce NaN or ±∞. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੵ.4.1 multiplication operation and to Haskell's (*) :: Double -> Double -> Double.
primitive.description: Floating-point multiplication.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.mulFloat64

primitive.comments: Arithmetic negation of a 32-bit signed integer. The result is 0 - x reduced modulo 2^32 and reinterpreted as a signed int32. The function is total but not injective at the boundary: negate(minBound) = minBound (i.e. negate(-2147483648) = -2147483648), because +2147483648 is not representable in int32. Corresponds to Haskell's negate :: Int32 -> Int32.
primitive.description: Negate an integer.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.negate

primitive.comments: Sign reversal of a float64. Equivalent to IEEE 754 ੵ.5.1 negate: flips the sign bit, so negate(ଐ) = ଐ (sign flips), negate(±∞) = ±∞ (sign flips), and negate(NaN) is a NaN (sign may flip; payload preserved). This is a bit-level operation that does not raise any floating-point exception. Corresponds to Haskell's negate :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: Negate a floating-point number.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.negateFloat64

primitive.comments: True if the argument is not divisible by 2 (i.e. x mod 2 ≠ 0), false otherwise. Total on all int32 inputs including negative numbers and minBound. Corresponds to Haskell's odd :: Int32 -> Bool.

literal.string: Test whether an integer is odd, defined as the negation of even.

primitive.description: Test whether an integer is odd.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.odd

primitive.comments: The mathematical constant π ≈ 3.141592653589793, the ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter, as the nearest representable float64. Corresponds to Haskell's pi :: Double.
primitive.description: The mathematical constant pi.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.pi

primitive.comments: pow(x, y) = x^y. Follows the IEEE 754 ੹.2 pow operation: pow(ଐ, y) for y < 0 is ±∞ (with division-by-zero exception); pow(ଐ, y) for y > 0 is ଐ if y is an odd integer, else +0; pow(1, y) = 1 for any y including NaN; pow(x, ଐ) = 1 for any x including NaN; pow(x, y) for negative x and non-integer y is NaN (with invalid-operation exception); pow(±∞, y) follows the limits in the usual way. Otherwise the result is x^y rounded to the nearest representable float64. Corresponds to Haskell's (**) :: Double -> Double -> Double.
primitive.description: Raise the first argument to the power of the second.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.pow

primitive.comments: range(a, b) returns the list [a, a+1, ..., b]. The range is inclusive at both ends; if a > b the result is the empty list (i.e. the range does not count downward). For a = b the result is the singleton [a]. The length of the result is max(0, b - a + 1). Corresponds to Haskell's enumFromTo :: Int32 -> Int32 -> [Int32], equivalent to the list-comprehension form [a..b].
primitive.description: Construct the inclusive integer range from the first to the second argument.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.range

primitive.comments: Round to the nearest integer value, returned as a float64, with ties rounded to the nearest even integer (banker's rounding). Equivalent to IEEE 754 ੵ.3.1 roundToIntegralTiesToEven: round(ଐ) = ଐ; round(±∞) = ±∞; round(NaN) is NaN. Note that the return type is float64, not an integer type, so the result can exceed the integer range. Corresponds to Haskell's fromIntegral . round :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: Round a floating-point number to the nearest integer-valued float.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.round

primitive.comments: roundFloat32(n, x) rounds the float32 value x to n decimal places using round-half-to-even. The result is the nearest float32 representation of x rounded to that decimal precision; if the exact decimal-rounded value is not representable in float32 (the usual case), the closest float32 is returned. Special values pass through: roundFloat32(n, ଐ) = ଐ; roundFloat32(n, ±∞) = ±∞; roundFloat32(n, NaN) is NaN. Negative n is supported in principle (rounding to powers of ten above 1); host implementations may differ on out-of-range n.
primitive.description: Round a float32 to the given number of decimal places.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.roundFloat32

primitive.comments: roundFloat64(n, x) rounds the float64 value x to n decimal places using round-half-to-even. The result is the nearest float64 representation of x rounded to that decimal precision; if the exact decimal-rounded value is not representable in float64 (the usual case), the closest float64 is returned. Special values pass through: roundFloat64(n, ଐ) = ଐ; roundFloat64(n, ±∞) = ±∞; roundFloat64(n, NaN) is NaN. Negative n is supported in principle (rounding to powers of ten above 1); host implementations may differ on out-of-range n.
primitive.description: Round a float64 to the given number of decimal places.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.roundFloat64

primitive.comments: signum(x) returns -1 if x < 0, 0 if x = 0, and 1 if x > 0. The function is total and satisfies the identity abs(x) * signum(x) = x for all int32 except minBound (where abs(minBound) * (-1) wraps to minBound rather than equalling -minBound, since +2147483648 is not representable). Corresponds to Haskell's signum :: Int32 -> Int32.
primitive.description: Return the sign of an integer as -1, 0, or 1.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.signum

primitive.comments: Sine of an angle in radians, correctly rounded for finite arguments. The result is in [-1, +1]; sin(ଐ) = ଐ; sin(±∞) is NaN (with invalid-operation exception in IEEE 754); sin(NaN) is NaN. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੹.2 sin operation and to Haskell's sin :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The sine of a floating-point number.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.sin

primitive.comments: Hyperbolic sine. sinh(ଐ) = ଐ; sinh(±∞) = ±∞; sinh(NaN) is NaN. Large-magnitude arguments overflow to ±∞. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੹.2 sinh operation and to Haskell's sinh :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The hyperbolic sine of a floating-point number.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.sinh

primitive.comments: IEEE 754 binary64 square root. The result is the value of √x correctly rounded to the nearest representable float64 under roundTiesToEven. sqrt(+0) = +0; sqrt(-0) = -0 (sign preserved); sqrt(x) for x < 0 (including -∞) is NaN (with invalid-operation exception); sqrt(+∞) = +∞; sqrt(NaN) is NaN. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੵ.4.1 squareRoot operation and to Haskell's sqrt :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The non-negative square root of a floating-point number.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.sqrt

primitive.comments: Two's-complement 32-bit signed integer subtraction. The result is x - y reduced modulo 2^32 and reinterpreted as a signed int32 (i.e. arithmetic wraps silently on overflow, with no exception raised). The operation is total. Corresponds to Haskell's (-) :: Int32 -> Int32 -> Int32.
primitive.description: Integer subtraction.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.sub

primitive.comments: IEEE 754 binary64 subtraction, defined as x + (-y). The result is correctly rounded to the nearest representable float64 under roundTiesToEven. Subtracting infinities of the same sign (+∞ - +∞ or -∞ - -∞) produces a NaN; subtracting any value involving NaN produces a NaN. The difference of two equal finite values is +0 (or -0 under round-toward-negative, which is not the default). The operation is total: it never raises, but it may produce NaN. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੵ.4.1 subtraction operation and to Haskell's (-) :: Double -> Double -> Double.
primitive.description: Floating-point subtraction.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.subFloat64

primitive.comments: Tangent of an angle in radians, correctly rounded for finite arguments. tan(ଐ) = ଐ; near odd multiples of π/2 the result has large magnitude but remains finite (no exception is raised); tan(±∞) is NaN (with invalid-operation exception in IEEE 754); tan(NaN) is NaN. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੹.2 tan operation and to Haskell's tan :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The tangent of a floating-point number.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.tan

primitive.comments: Hyperbolic tangent. The result is in [-1, +1]; tanh(ଐ) = ଐ; tanh(±∞) = ଑; tanh(NaN) is NaN. Corresponds to the IEEE 754 ੹.2 tanh operation and to Haskell's tanh :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: The hyperbolic tangent of a floating-point number.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.tanh

primitive.comments: Round toward zero (truncate the fractional part), returned as a float64. Equivalent to IEEE 754 ੵ.3.1 roundToIntegralTowardZero: truncate(ଐ) = ଐ; truncate(±∞) = ±∞; truncate(NaN) is NaN; sign of the result matches the sign of the argument (so truncate(-0.7) = -0, truncate(+0.7) = +0). Note that the return type is float64, not an integer type, so the result can exceed the integer range. Corresponds to Haskell's fromIntegral . truncate :: Double -> Double.
primitive.description: Truncate a floating-point number toward zero, as a float.
primitive.name: hydra.lib.math.truncate
